title: Designing a Vertical Micro Garden For Urban Households
overview_text: A sustainable engineering initiative evaluating methods for urban agriculture in high-rise buildings like the Sahaspura housing project. Using a weighted decision matrix, the team evaluated various solutions and proposed a cost-effective, scalable rooftop vertical micro-garden utilizing local materials.
problem_statement: In modern urban areas, particularly high-rise housing projects, a severe lack of space prevents residents from growing traditional gardens. This limits access to fresh food, increases reliance on street vendors, and disconnects the community from urban agriculture.
objectives: Propose appropriate solutions to enhance urban agriculture benefits for people, the environment, and the economy.
Evaluate alternatives using a weighted matrix covering environmental impact, cost, and technical feasibility.
Design a comprehensive resource and budget plan for a 10m² rooftop garden.

diagrams.caption: Weighted evaluation matrix for proposed solutions.
